title: Customer Profile API Migration

summary.overview: This PR migrates the customer profile API from v1 to v2, introducing a new normalized data model and updated REST endpoints. While the new schema design is sound, the migration lacks a rollback strategy and introduces several breaking changes to existing consumers. The scope of changes across 14 files warrants careful staged rollout with backward-compatibility shims.

riskAssessment.summary: The migration introduces breaking changes to the profile API contract affecting multiple downstream consumers. The absence of a rollback mechanism for the data migration poses a significant risk to production data integrity.
items: No rollback migration script provided for the v2 schema changes
Breaking changes to GET /api/profile response shape without versioned endpoint support
Downstream services consuming profile events have not been updated for the new payload format
Data migration runs synchronously which could cause extended downtime for large datasets
Missing validation for partially migrated profiles during the transition window

strengths: Well-normalized data model in v2 eliminates data duplication present in the v1 schema
Comprehensive migration guide documentation included in the PR
Strong test coverage for the new MigrationService with edge case handling

issues.message: Data migration has no rollback mechanism. If the migration fails midway, partially migrated records will be left in an inconsistent state with no way to revert.
issues.recommendation: Implement a reversible migration pattern with a corresponding down migration script and transaction boundaries around batch operations.

issues.message: The v2 response shape removes the 'fullName' field and restructures 'address' into a nested object, breaking all existing API consumers without a deprecation period.
issues.recommendation: Introduce API versioning via URL path or Accept header, maintain the v1 endpoint with a deprecation notice, and provide a minimum 90-day migration window.

issues.message: The ALTER TABLE statements lack explicit lock timeout configuration, which could cause table-level locks on large production datasets during migration.
issues.recommendation: Use online DDL strategies such as pt-online-schema-change or gh-ost to avoid extended table locks during the schema migration.

issues.message: Profile update operations do not validate whether the record has been fully migrated to v2 before applying v2-format writes, risking data corruption during the transition period.
issues.recommendation: Add a migration status check before write operations and route to the appropriate handler based on the profile's current schema version.

issues.message: Validation error messages expose internal field mapping details that could leak implementation specifics to API consumers.
issues.recommendation: Use consumer-facing error codes and messages that map to the public API contract rather than internal schema field names.

issues.message: The version detection logic falls back to v2 when no version header is present, which will break existing clients that do not send version headers.
issues.recommendation: Default to v1 for unversioned requests and require explicit opt-in to v2 via the API-Version header.

recommendations: Create a reversible down migration script and validate it against a production-sized dataset clone before deployment
Implement API versioning with concurrent v1 and v2 endpoint support during the transition period
Add a feature flag to control the migration rollout and enable per-tenant staged migration
Run the data migration as an async background job with progress tracking rather than a synchronous operation
Set up monitoring dashboards for migration progress, error rates, and partially migrated profile counts
Coordinate with downstream service teams to update their consumers before deprecating v1 endpoints
